problem: `kb/experiments` is ingesting options-MC artifacts generated from pytest temp-chain fixtures (e.g., `/private/var/.../pytest-of-forge/...`). This contaminates production experiment history with non-production inputs, breaks dedupe assumptions via synthetic canonical hashes, and can degrade profitability/reliability by letting test scenarios influence downstream ranking/monitoring.

pattern_check.rule: chain snapshot path contains pytest temp fixture markers (e.g., 'pytest-of-' or test tmp dirs)

required_change: Enforce an input-environment contract: artifacts written to production `kb/experiments` must use approved live/sim data roots; reject/quarantine test fixture paths.
Add `run_context` classification (`prod|staging|test`) derived from input provenance; only `prod` may publish into `kb/experiments`.
On non-prod detection, fail closed with machine-readable status (e.g., `NO_ACTION_NONPROD_INPUT`) and write to a separate test/staging artifact sink (or skip publication).
Add provenance guardrails so canonical hash/dedupe logic ignores blocked non-prod runs and cannot churn production telemetry.
acceptance_tests: Unit test: snapshot path containing `pytest-of-` is classified `run_context=test` and blocked from publishing to `kb/experiments`.
Unit test: approved production path (or null live feed path) remains `run_context=prod` and publishes normally.
Integration test: replay pytest fixture inputs; command returns `NO_ACTION_NONPROD_INPUT` (or equivalent), and no new file appears under `kb/experiments`.
Integration test: blocked non-prod runs do not increment production telemetry counters used by dedupe/circuit-breaker logic.
Regression test: production cron path still emits expected artifacts and schema when inputs are valid prod provenance.
constraints: Do not weaken existing DQ fail-closed behavior.
Do not break schema compatibility for existing prod artifacts; additive fields/statuses only.
Keep provenance checks deterministic and auditable.
